claimText: Nano fertilizers use nanomaterials measuring 100 nanometres or less to encapsulate nutrients, allowing for their controlled release and slow diffusion into the soil.
evidenceExcerpt: Nano fertilizers encapsulate nutrients within nanomaterials (100 nm or less) for controlled release and slow diffusion into soil.
examUse: Statement-based question on the mechanism and size criteria of nano fertilizers.

claimText: IFFCO launched Nano Liquid Urea in 2021 and Nano-DAP in 2023, and is establishing its first overseas plant in Brazil.
evidenceExcerpt: IFFCO, which launched Nano Liquid Urea (2021) and Nano-DAP (2023), is setting up its first overseas plant in Brazil.
examUse: Factual statement in a multi-statement question about India's fertilizer sector or IFFCO's global expansion.
distractorHooks: First overseas plant in USA or Africa
Launched by a private foreign MNC instead of IFFCO

claimText: Scientists at TERI developed a green nano sulphur using biological agents such as plant-promoting bacteria.
evidenceExcerpt: TERI scientists developed a green nano sulphur using biological agents (plant-promoting bacteria)
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question identifying the institution behind green nano sulphur or the method of its production.
distractorHooks: Synthesized using heavy metals or toxic chemicals instead of biological agents

claimText: The green nano sulphur developed by TERI possesses antibacterial and insecticidal properties and has been shown to increase mustard yield.
evidenceExcerpt: which has antibacterial and insecticidal properties and increased mustard yield by 30-40%.
examUse: Statement-based question on the dual benefits of nano sulphur as both a nutrient and a pest control agent.
distractorHooks: Only acts as a nutrient with no pest control properties
Decreases crop yield due to toxicity
